channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI unleashes o3-mini reasoning model
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released the latest model in its reasoning series, o3-mini, both in ChatGPT and its application programming interface (API). It had been in preview since December 2024.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas. "We'll be doing pharmaceuticals. Importantly in drugs and medicines, etc. All forms of medicine and pharmaceuticals," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: What is NaaS? Providers, delivery models, and benefits explained
item.description: Network as a service (NaaS) is a cloud service model that’s designed to let enterprise IT professionals order network infrastructure components from a menu of options, have them configured to fit their business needs, and have the whole thing delivered, running and managed in a matter of hours inste
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia's DLSS 4 is amazing – here's what AMD's FSR 4 needs to do to take it on
item.description: Team Green has succeeded in leaving me excited for the future of upscaling in games with DLSS 4, and I hope AMD can do the same with FSR 4.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Renault Group strengthens decarbonisation commitment
item.description: Renault Group is committed to taking actions that help reduce its carbon footprint. By using HP IT Asset Disposition Service, the company’s end-of-use devices are securely collected, sanitised, and restored or responsibly recycled by HP.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Apple Vision Pro is finding a home in healthcare
item.description: In early 2024, Ryan Broderick, a surgeon at UC San Diego Health, was commiserating with some colleagues about the profusion of screens in today’s operating rooms. Though the displays provided essential guidance for minimally invasive operations in progress, they also added complexity.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Grim memories resurface after DNA advances lead to arrest of suspect in Hawaii teen's 1977 murder
item.description: The unsolved death of a teenager in 1977 haunted students and staff at Honolulu's McKinley High School for nearly half a century.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After a week of DeepSeek freakout, doubts and mysteries remain
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla’s Q4 results fell short of Wall Street’s forecasts. Here’s why
item.description: Tesla’s fourth-quarter adjusted profits rose slightly amid a big push to sell its electric vehicles with offers of zero financing and other incentives, but the results still fell short of Wall Street forecasts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How federal land could generate lots of electricity
item.description: "We estimate 10 to 20 million homes could be served from the electricity that are produced from that renewable energy," said Trieu Mai. The post How federal land could generate lots of electricity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cisco touts ‘Internet of Agents’ for secure AI agent collaboration
item.description: When AI agents begin to proliferate, a new, open structure will be needed so they can securely communicate and collaborate together to solve complex problems, suggests Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA unveils AI Essentials training resource
item.description: Artificial intelligence is changing how work gets done, creating new technology roles, and transforming the entire employment landscape.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Mattermost’s Bill Anderson, Matthew Heideman on how DoD benefits from platform approach to collaboration
item.description: Mattermost’s tech experts explain how they create the “connective tissues of information, people and data” for this secure collaboration platform.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Browser Use: An open-source AI agent to automate web-based tasks
item.description: Author’s note: The generative AI revolution has sparked an explosion of open-source tools that fundamentally transform how developers build and deploy AI-powered applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Zuckerberg hails Trump administration on Meta earnings call
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Wednesday praised President Trump’s administration and said he looks forward to “redefining” the relationship the tech giant has with governments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fake toll road texts sweep America as Chinese scammers target US drivers
item.description: Toll road text scam: Fake messages claim unpaid fees, seek payment via fraudulent links. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this scam is becoming increasingly sophisticated and widespread.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Share Specific Moments from YouTube Videos: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Learn how to share specific moments from YouTube videos on your Android device using the new simplified method or the previous steps for older versions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4
